rules.description: Atomic final JSON of a health script run, covering all three phases (pre-detection / remediation / post-detection) emitted by IME after the policy completes. Single source of truth for health-script outcomes — replaces the line-by-line HS-COMPLIANCE/HS-EXITCODE/HS-STDOUT/HS-STDERR patterns. The end-anchor and [\s\S] character class are defensive against future format changes and multi-line CMTrace buffering.

Detects when a Proactive Remediation (Health Script) starts processing. Captures PolicyId and PolicyType (6 = remediation). Drives the live script_started event for UI progress indication; the consolidated final result for the same PolicyId arrives later via HS-NEW-RESULT.
Captures the Intune Management Extension agent version number.
Detects when an app from a previous ESP phase is detected as already installed, so it should be ignored in the current phase.
Detects enforcement success in a non-current phase, indicating an app completed in a previous phase should be ignored.
Detects an MSI-based Win32 app via the 'MSI App <guid>' phrase in the ESP retry configuration line. Marks AppType as 'MSI' for that app id.
Detects a WinGet-backed Store app by any [Win32App][WinGetApp] handler log line (Detection/Applicability/Operation). Marks AppType as 'WinGet' for that app id.

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ReportingManager\] Detection state for app with id: (?<id>{GUID}).*?"DetectedIdentityVersion":\{[^}]*?"NewValue":"(?<appVersion>[^"]+)"
rules.description: Captures the product version IME detected after an app install (e.g. "11.2.1787.0"). Source: [ReportingManager] Detection state update with DetectedIdentityVersion.NewValue. Used by App Dashboard to show version-level failure breakdowns.

Captures the install attempt number for the current app. IME logs 'Execute retry 0' for the first attempt and increments on each retry. Reported as attempt+1 so retry 0 becomes AttemptNumber=1 (human-friendly: first attempt).
Detects when app content decryption fails, indicating a corrupted or tampered download.

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ReportingManager\] Detection state for app with id: {GUID} has been updated\. Report delta: \{""DetectionState"":\{""OldValue"":null,""NewValue"":""Installed""
rules.description: Detects when an app's detection state transitions to Installed (new ReportingManager format).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[DetectionActionHandler\] Detection for policy with id: {GUID} resulted in action status: Success and detection state: Detected
rules.description: Detects when an app's detection action handler reports successful detection (app is installed).
Detects when an app is detected as installed (old log format).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[DetectionActionHandler\] Detection for policy with id: (?<id>{GUID}) resulted in action status: Success and detection state: (?<detection>Detected|NotDetected)
rules.description: Captures the detection rule result after IME runs detection for an app. 'Detected' means the detection rule matched (app is installed), 'NotDetected' means it did not. Excludes [SimulatedDetectionActionHandler] which fires during applicability pre-checks.
Captures Delivery Optimization telemetry JSON after a DO download completes. Contains file size, peer caching stats, download mode, and duration.
Detects when a Delivery Optimization download times out for a specific app (identified by GUID in filename).
Detects when a Delivery Optimization download times out (generic, uses current app context).

rules.pattern: \[StatusService\] Downloading app \(id = {GUID}.*?\) via (?<tech>\w+), bytes (?<bytes>\w+)/(?<ofbytes>\w+) for user
rules.description: Captures app download progress including technology (DO/CDN), bytes downloaded, and total bytes.

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ReportingManager\] Execution state for app with id: {GUID} has been updated\. Report delta: \{""EnforcementState"":\{""OldValue"":""InProgressDownloadCompleted"",""NewValue"":""Success""
rules.description: Detects when an app's enforcement state transitions from InProgressDownloadCompleted to Success.
Detects when enforcement state is set to Success for the current app (old log format).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ReportingManager\] Download state for app with id: {GUID} has been updated\. Report delta: \{""EnforcementState"":\{""OldValue"":""InProgress"",""NewValue"":""ErrorDownloadingContent""
rules.description: Detects when an app's enforcement state transitions to ErrorDownloadingContent.
Detects when enforcement state is set to Error for the current app (old log format).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\] Didn't get content info from service
rules.description: Detects when IME cannot retrieve content info from the Intune service for the current app.

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ReportingManager\] Execution state for app with id: {GUID} has been updated\. Report delta: \{""EnforcementState"":\{""OldValue"":""(?<from>\w+)"",""NewValue"":""(?<to>\w+)""
rules.description: Captures enforcement state transitions via ReportingManager. Only triggers error when 'to' value indicates an error state.
Detects when an app installation times out.

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\] Admin did NOT set mapping for lpExitCode\W+(?<exitCode>\d+) of app\W+{GUID}
rules.description: Detects when an app installer returned an exit code that has no mapping configured in Intune.
Detects ESP phase transitions reported by IME Win32App processing (e.g., DeviceSetup, AccountSetup).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[EspManager\] Updating ESP tracked install status from (?<from>\w+) to (?<to>\w+) for application {GUID}\.
rules.description: Captures ESP tracked install status transitions (e.g., InProgress to Installed) for a specific app.
Captures the installer exit code (lpExitCode) for the current app.
Captures the Win32 HRESULT for the current app.
Detects when IME impersonates a user context for app installation.

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ExecutionActionHandler\] Handler invoked with execution type: Install for policy with id: {GUID}
rules.description: Detects when the IME execution handler starts installing an app.
Detects when the Win32 app installer process is launched in a user or system session.

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ActionProcessor\] No action required for app with id: {GUID}
rules.description: Detects when IME determines no action is required for an app (already in desired state).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ApplicabilityActionHandler\] Applicability check for policy with id: {GUID} resulted in action status: Success and applicability state: NotApplicable
rules.description: Detects when an app's applicability check returns NotApplicable (requirements not met).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ReportingManager\] Applicability state for app with id: {GUID} has been updated\. Report delta: \{""ApplicabilityState"":\{""OldValue"":null,""NewValue"":""ScriptRequirementRuleNotMet""
rules.description: Detects when an app's applicability state is set to ScriptRequirementRuleNotMet (PowerShell requirement script returned false).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ReportingManager\] Desired state for app with id: {GUID} has been updated\. Report delta: \{""DesiredState"":\{""OldValue"":null,""NewValue"":""None""
rules.description: Detects when an app's desired state is set to None (not targeted or not applicable).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ReportingManager\] Desired state for app with id: {GUID} has been updated\. Report delta: \{""DesiredState"":\{""OldValue"":null,""NewValue"":""NotPresent""
rules.description: Detects when an app's desired state is set to NotPresent (targeted for uninstall or not required).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ActionProcessor\] App with id: {GUID}, targeted intent: RequiredInstall, and enforceability: Enforceable has projected enforcement classification: NotApplicableOrNotTargeted with desired state: None\.
rules.description: Detects when an app with RequiredInstall intent is classified as NotApplicableOrNotTargeted (new log format).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[ActionProcessor\] App with id: {GUID}, effective intent: RequiredInstall, and enforceability: Enforceable has projected enforcement classification: NotApplicableOrNotTargeted with desired state: None\.
rules.description: Detects when an app with effective RequiredInstall intent is classified as NotApplicableOrNotTargeted.
Captures the JSON policies list discovered by IME, used to build the app inventory.
Detects Windows session change events (logon, logoff, lock, unlock) reported by IME.
Detects when IME begins processing an app with its dependency chain, setting it as the current app.
Detects when the IME ExecManager starts processing a targeted app, setting it as current.
Detects the start of app presentation step in IME, setting the app as current.
Detects when IME sets the working directory for an app's staging folder, setting it as current.
Detects when the IME agent receives a shutdown signal, postponing the current app installation.
Detects when an app's applicability check returns NotMet (old log format with lookaheads).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\] app\W+{GUID}\b.*?\bis a dependency\b.*?\bDetect Only mode\b.*?\bfinished processing
rules.description: Detects when a dependency app in Detect Only mode finishes processing (no install needed).
Detects when an already-detected app is skipped for further processing steps (old log format).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\]\[V3Processor\] All apps in the subgraph are not applicable due to assignment filters\. Skipping processing\.
rules.description: Detects when all apps in a subgraph are skipped due to assignment filter exclusions.

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\] Detected app {GUID} and intent is to install, skip applicability check
rules.description: Detects when an app targeted for install is already detected/installed, so the install is skipped (old log format).

rules.pattern: \[Win32App\] Not detected app {GUID} and intent is to un-install, skip applicability check
rules.description: Detects when an app targeted for uninstall is not detected, so the uninstall is skipped (old log format).
Detects when an app is not detected and uninstall is skipped (alternate old log format).
Detects when the Intune Management Extension (IME) agent starts up.
Detects when V3Processor starts a new subgraph, cancelling any stuck app and setting the new app as current.
Detects successful token acquisition, indicating the AccountSetup ESP phase has started.
Captures the app name from ExecManager processing log (old format: name before id).
Captures the app name from ProcessAppWithDependencies log entry.
Captures the app name from the Processing app log entry.
Detects when IME completes a user session, indicating enrollment has finished for that user.
Captures the Win32 app installation state string from instance ID tracking (old log format).
Captures the Win32 app ESP state (e.g., Installed, InProgress) for a specific app.

rules.pattern: \[WinGetMessageProcessor\] Processing Progress for app = {GUID} and user = .*?- Operation Phase = Installing
rules.description: Detects WinGet app installation progress with Operation Phase = Installing (format variant 1).

rules.pattern: \[WinGetMessageProcessor\] Processing Progress for app = {GUID}.*?- Operation Phase = Installing
rules.description: Detects WinGet app installation progress with Operation Phase = Installing (format variant 2).
Captures error output from AgentExecutor.log when a platform script produces stderr content.
Captures the PowerShell exit code from AgentExecutor.log.
Captures both stdout and stderr from AgentExecutor.log when a platform script completes. The output group contains stdout, the error group contains stderr.
Detects platform script identification in AgentExecutor.log. Captures PolicyId from the script file path for stdout/stderr correlation.
Captures whether a PowerShell script runs in machine (System) or user session context.
Captures the PowerShell exit code when script execution completes in the IME log.
Detects when a platform PowerShell script file is generated by IME. Captures the PolicyId from the script file path.

rules.pattern: \[PowerShell\] User Id = [a-z0-9\-]+, Policy id = (?<id>[a-z0-9]{8}-[a-z0-9]{4}-[a-z0-9]{4}-[a-z0-9]{4}-[a-z0-9]{12}), policy result = (?<result>\w+)
rules.description: Captures the final result (Success/Failed) of a platform PowerShell script execution with its PolicyId.
